author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm XXII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To the end, for the taking up of the morning, a psalm of David.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To the end,” for His own resurrection, the Lord Jesus Christ Himself speaketh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For in the morning on the first day of the week was His resurrection, whereby He was taken up, into eternal life, “Over whom death shall have no more dominion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now what follows is spoken in the person of The Crucified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For from the head of this Psalm are the words, which He cried out, whilst hanging on the Cross, sustaining also the person of the old man, whose mortality He bare.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For our old man was nailed together with Him to the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O God, my God, look upon me, why hast Thou forsaken me far from my salvation?” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Far removed from my salvation: for “salvation is far from sinners.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The words of my sins.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For these are not the words of righteousness, but of my sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For it is the old man nailed to the Cross that speaks, ignorant even of the reason why God hath forsaken him: or else it may be thus, The words of my sins are far from my salvation.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My God, I will cry unto Thee in the daytime, and Thou wilt not hear” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My God, I will cry unto Thee in the prosperous circumstances of this life, that they be not changed; and Thou wilt not hear, because I shall cry unto Thee in the words of my sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And in the night-season, and not to my folly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And so in the adversities of this life will I cry to Thee for prosperity; and in like manner Thou wilt not hear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this Thou doest not to my folly, but rather that I may have wisdom to know what Thou wouldest have me cry for, not with the words of sins out of longing for life temporal, but with the words of turning to Thee for life eternal.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou dwellest in the holy place, O Thou praise of Israel” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Thou dwellest in the holy place, and therefore wilt not hear the unclean words of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The “praise” of him that seeth Thee; not of him who hath sought his own praise in tasting of the forbidden fruit, that on the opening of his bodily eyes he should endeavour to hide himself from Thy sight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Our Fathers hoped in Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the righteous, namely, who sought not their own praise, but Thine.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They hoped in Thee, and Thou deliveredst them” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They cried unto Thee, and were saved.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They cried unto Thee, not in the words of sins, from which salvation is far; and therefore were they saved.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They hoped in Thee, and were not confounded” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They hoped in Thee,” and their hope did not deceive them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they placed it not in themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But I am a worm, and no man” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But I, speaking now not in the person of Adam, but I in My own person, Jesus Christ, was born without human generation in the flesh, that I might be as man beyond men; that so at least human pride might deign to imitate My humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The scorn of men, and outcast of the people.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In which humility I was made the scorn of men, so as that it should be said, as a reproachful railing, “Be thou His disciple:” and that the people despise Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All that saw Me laughed Me to scorn” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All that saw Me derided Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And spake with the lips, and shook the head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they spoke, not with the heart, but with the lips.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they shook their head in derision, saying, “He trusted in the Lord, let Him deliver Him:” “let Him save Him, since He desireth Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These were their words; but they were spoken “with the lips.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Since Thou art He who drew Me out of the womb” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since Thou art He who drew Me, not only out of that Virgin womb (for this is the law of all men’s birth, that they be drawn out of the womb), but also out of the womb of the Jewish nation; by the darkness whereof he is covered, and not yet born into the light of Christ, whosoever places his salvation in the carnal observance of the Sabbath, and of circumcision, and the like.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My hope from My mother’s breasts.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My hope,” O God, not from the time when I began to be fed by the milk of the Virgin’s breasts; for it was even before; but from the breasts of the Synagogue, as I have said, out of the womb, Thou hast drawn Me, that I should not suck in the customs of the flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I have been strengthened in Thee from the womb” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the womb of the Synagogue, which did not carry Me, but threw Me out: but I fell not, for Thou heldest me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From My mother’s womb Thou art My God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “From My mother’s womb: My mother’s womb did not cause that, as a babe, I should be forgetful of Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Thou art My God,” “depart not from Me; for trouble is hard at hand” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art, therefore, My God, depart not from Me; for trouble is nigh unto Me; for it is in My body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For there is none to help.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For who helpeth, if Thou helpest not?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Many calves came about Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The multitude of the wanton populace came about Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fat bulls closed Me in” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And their leaders, glad at My oppression, “closed Me in.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They opened their mouth upon Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They opened their mouth upon Me, not out of Thy Scripture, but of their own lusts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “As a ravening and roaring lion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As a lion, whose ravening is, that I was taken and led; and whose roaring, “Crucify, Crucify.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I was poured out like water, and all My bones were scattered” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I was poured out like water,” when My persecutors fell: and through fear, the stays of My body, that is, the Church, My disciples were scattered from Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My heart became as melting wax, in the midst of my belly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My wisdom, which was written of Me in the sacred books, was, as if hard and shut up, not understood: but after that the fire of My Passion was applied, it was, as if melted, manifested, and entertained in the memory of My Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “My strength dried up as a potsherd” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My strength dried up by My Passion; not as hay, but a potsherd, which is made stronger by fire.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My tongue cleaved to My jaws.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And they, through whom I was soon to speak, kept My precepts in their hearts.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And Thou broughtest Me down to the dust of death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And to the ungodly appointed to death, whom the wind casteth forth as dust from the face of the earth, Thou broughtest Me down.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For many dogs came about Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many came about Me barking, not for truth, but for custom.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The council of the malignant came about Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The council of the malignant besieged Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They pierced My hands and feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They pierced with nails My hands and feet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They numbered distinctly all My bones” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They numbered distinctly all My bones, while extended on the wood of the Cross.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Yea, these same regarded, and beheld Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yea, these same, that is, unchanged, regarded and beheld Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They divided My garments for themselves, and cast the lot upon My vesture” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But Thou, O Lord, withhold not Thy help far from Me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But Thou, O Lord, raise Me up again, not as the rest of men, at the end of the world, but immediately.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Look to My defence.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Look,” that they in no wise hurt Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver My soul from the sword.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Deliver My soul” from the tongue of dissension.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My only One from the hand of the dog” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And from the power of the people, barking after their custom, deliver My Church.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Save Me from the lion’s mouth:” save Me from the mouth of the kingdom of this world: “and my humility from the horns of the unicorns” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And from the loftiness of the proud, exalting themselves to special pre-eminence, and enduring no partakers, save My humility.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will declare Thy name to My brethren” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will declare Thy name to the humble, and to My Brethren that love one another as they have been beloved by Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the midst of the Church will I sing of Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the midst of the Church will I with rejoicing preach Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Ye that fear the Lord, praise Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Ye that fear the Lord,” seek not your own praise, but “praise Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All ye seed of Jacob, magnify Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All ye seed of him whom the elder shall serve, magnify Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let all the seed of Israel fear Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let all who have been born to a new life, and restored to the vision of God “fear Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Since He hath not despised, nor disregarded the prayer of the poor man” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Since He hath not despised the prayer, not of him who, crying unto God in the words of sins was loath to overpass a vain life, but the prayer of the poor man, not swollen up with transitory pomps.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Nor hath He turned away His face from Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As from him who said, I will cry unto Thee, but Thou wilt not hear.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And when I cried unto Him He heard Me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With Thee is My praise” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For I seek not Mine own praise, for Thou art My praise, who dwellest in the holy place; and, praise of Israel, Thou hearest The Holy One now beseeching Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In the great Church I will confess Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the Church of the whole world “I will confess Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I will offer My vows in the sight of them that fear Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will offer the sacraments of My Body and Blood in the sight of them that fear Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The poor shall eat, and be filled” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The humble and the despisers of the world shall eat, and imitate Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For so they will neither desire this world’s abundance, nor fear its want.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And they shall praise the Lord, who seek Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the praise of the Lord is the pouring out of that fulness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Their hearts shall live for ever and ever.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For that food is the food of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the borders of the earth shall remember themselves, and be turned to the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They shall remember themselves: for, by the Gentiles, born in death and bent on outward things, God had been forgotten; and then shall all the borders of the earth be turned to the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And all the kindreds of the nations shall worship in His sight.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And all the kindreds of the nations shall worship in their own consciences.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the kingdom is the Lord’s, and He shall rule over the nations” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the kingdom is the Lord’s, not proud men’s: and He shall rule over the nations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “All the rich of the earth have eaten, and worshipped” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The rich of the earth too have eaten the Body of their Lord’s humiliation, and though they have not, as the poor, been filled even to imitation, yet they have worshipped.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “In His sight shall fall all that descend to earth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He alone seeth how all they fall, who abandoning a heavenly conversation, make choice, on earth, to appear happy to men, who see not their fall.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My Soul shall live to Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And My Soul, which in the contempt of this world seems to men as it were to die, shall live, not to itself, but to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And My seed shall serve Him” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And My deeds, or they who through Me believe on Him, shall serve Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The generation to come shall be declared to the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The generation of the New Testament shall be declared to the honour of the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And the heavens shall declare His righteousness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And the Evangelists shall declare His righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “To a people that shall be born, whom the Lord hath made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To a people that shall be born to the Lord through faith.
